error.not_available_body: It may still be saving. Give it a moment, then try again.
error.generic_body: This object couldn't be opened. Go back to your objects and try again.

title: Objects
hint: Manage the images your stories reference. Add metadata, verify IIIF tiles, and import external sources.
add_objects_sync_desc: Import objects that have already been added to your GitHub repository.
add_objects_upload_desc: Upload image files from your computer. A site rebuild will be needed to generate IIIF tiles.
add_objects_iiif_desc: Link to an image hosted by a museum, library, or other IIIF-compatible institution.
empty_description: Your objects library is empty. Sync from your repo to import existing objects, or add an external IIIF object manually.
field_title_help: The name of this object as it will appear on your published site.
field_creator_help: The person or organization that created the original object.
field_description_help: A brief description of the object, shown on the objects page of your published site.
field_period_help: The historical period this object belongs to (e.g. "Colonial period", "19th century").
field_year_help: In YYYY format — for filtering and sorting chronologically.
field_object_type_help: The medium, genre, or format of this object (e.g. "Map", "Photograph", "Engraving", "Audio recording", "Video").
field_subjects_help: Topics or themes related to this object, separated by commas.
field_source_help: The repository, archive, or collection where this object is held.
field_credit_help: Attribution line shown on the published site (e.g. "Courtesy of UCSB Library").
field_alt_text_placeholder: Describe what this object shows or contains...
field_alt_text_help: Describe this object for visitors using screen readers. Good descriptions mention the subject, action, and important details. For images, avoid phrases like "Image of" — screen readers already announce it as an image. For audio or video, describe what the recording contains. Example: "Hand-drawn map of a coastal town with ships in the harbor and mountains in the background."
sync_description: Check the changes you would like to sync (pull) from GitHub.
sync_no_changes: No changes detected. Your objects are up to date.
sync_missing_warning: These objects exist in the compositor but not in the repo. They may have been deleted from objects.csv.
sync_unregistered_hint: These image files are in the objects folder but not yet listed on this Compositor or the project spreadsheets. Register them to add metadata.
add_iiif_error_fetch: Could not reach the manifest URL. Check the URL and try again.
add_iiif_error_invalid: The URL does not point to a valid IIIF manifest.
add_iiif_error_parse: The manifest could not be parsed. It may use an unsupported format.
external_url_placeholder: Paste a YouTube, Vimeo, Google Drive, or audio link
external_unrecognised: Link not recognized yet — paste a YouTube, Vimeo, Google Drive, or audio URL
filter_placeholder: Filter by title, creator, year, or ID
viewer_tiles_unavailable: IIIF tiles have not been generated for this object yet. Rebuild the site to create them.
delete_description: Are you sure you want to delete '{{title}}'?
staleHeadError: The repository has changed since your last sync. Please re-sync before saving.
commitFailed: Saving to GitHub failed. Your changes were not committed — try again.
sheetsWarning: Google Sheets is currently enabled for this site. Saving will disable it so the build uses your compositor data instead.
sheetsReversible: You can switch back to Google Sheets at any time by setting enabled: true under google_sheets in your site's _config.yml.
buildQueued: Build queued — waiting for GitHub Actions...
objectSavedHint: The image has been committed to your repository. A site rebuild is needed to generate the IIIF tiles — you can trigger one from the object's detail page.
buildFailed: Build failed. Check GitHub Actions for details.
commitModal.description: To register {{count}} new object(s) we need to update your site's objects.csv on GitHub. This will trigger a build and produce the IIIF tiles you need to work with these images in the compositor.
commitModal.urlMismatch: Your site's URL in _config.yml doesn't match your GitHub Pages deployment.
commitModal.urlFix: We'll correct this automatically so the IIIF tiles work properly.
commitModal.insertFailedBody: Your images are safely committed to your site's repository, but registering them in the compositor failed. Retrying is safe — nothing will be duplicated.
upload_drop_hint: JPG, PNG, TIFF — up to 25 MB each, 10 images max
upload_object_id_help: Auto-generated from filename. You can edit it.
upload_alt_help: Describes the image for screen readers
upload_error_size: File must be 25 MB or smaller
upload_error_generic: Upload failed. Check your connection and try again.
upload_error_stale: The repo has changed. Sync and try again.
upload_error_payload: File too large for GitHub. Try a smaller image.
upload_error_missing_data: Something went wrong preparing the upload. Close the dialog and try again.
upload_error_invalid_id: The object ID can only contain lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.
upload_error_no_project: No active project found. Reload the page and try again.
upload_error_forbidden: Only the project convenor can upload images.
sync_error_toast: Sync failed. Check your repository on GitHub and try again.
upload_dismiss_confirm: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to close?
upload_id_collision_hint: This ID already exists — the server will generate a unique one.
upload_error_too_many: Too many files — you can add up to {{max}} more
